title: Genetics, Reproduction, and Behavior Practice Test 3
description: A third mixed practice set with genetics, reproduction, and behavior analysis.

questions.prompt: What does it mean if a trait is recessive?
questions.questionText: What does it mean if a trait is recessive?
choices: It appears only when both alleles are recessive
It always appears whenever it is present
It is caused by the environment instead of genes
It can only be found in plants
options: It appears only when both alleles are recessive
It always appears whenever it is present
It is caused by the environment instead of genes
It can only be found in plants
questions.correctAnswer: It appears only when both alleles are recessive
questions.explanation: A recessive trait shows only when no dominant allele masks it. The other choices describe dominance or make false claims about cause or where traits occur.

questions.prompt: Which choice describes a phenotype?
questions.questionText: Which choice describes a phenotype?
choices: freckles visible on a person's face
the allele pair Ff
the letters in a Punnett square
a recessive allele hidden by a dominant allele
options: freckles visible on a person's face
the allele pair Ff
the letters in a Punnett square
a recessive allele hidden by a dominant allele
questions.correctAnswer: freckles visible on a person's face
questions.explanation: Phenotype is the trait you can observe, such as freckles. Ff is a genotype, and the other choices describe inheritance details rather than the visible trait.

questions.prompt: Which trait is inherited rather than acquired?
questions.questionText: Which trait is inherited rather than acquired?
choices: natural blood type
a sunburn
a scar from surgery
calluses from playing guitar
options: natural blood type
a sunburn
a scar from surgery
calluses from playing guitar
questions.correctAnswer: natural blood type
questions.explanation: Blood type is inherited genetically. Sunburns, scars, and calluses develop through experiences during life.

questions.prompt: A newborn foal stands shortly after it is born without being taught. This behavior is:
questions.questionText: A newborn foal stands shortly after it is born without being taught. This behavior is:
choices: instinct
conditioning
observational learning
playing
options: instinct
conditioning
observational learning
playing
questions.correctAnswer: instinct
questions.explanation: Standing soon after birth without teaching is inherited instinct. The other options are learned behaviors that depend on experience.

questions.prompt: Which statement is true about sexual reproduction?
questions.questionText: Which statement is true about sexual reproduction?
choices: It combines genetic material from two parents
It always creates identical offspring
It happens only in animals
It never involves seeds in plants
options: It combines genetic material from two parents
It always creates identical offspring
It happens only in animals
It never involves seeds in plants
questions.correctAnswer: It combines genetic material from two parents
questions.explanation: Sexual reproduction mixes genes from two parents. The other choices incorrectly claim no variation, limit it to animals, or deny plant seed reproduction.

questions.prompt: Which example best shows budding?
questions.questionText: Which example best shows budding?
choices: A yeast cell grows a small outgrowth that becomes a new cell
A fern releases spores
A bacterium splits into two equal cells
A turtle performs a mating dance
options: A yeast cell grows a small outgrowth that becomes a new cell
A fern releases spores
A bacterium splits into two equal cells
A turtle performs a mating dance
questions.correctAnswer: A yeast cell grows a small outgrowth that becomes a new cell
questions.explanation: Budding produces a smaller growth from the parent that becomes a new organism. Binary fission is equal splitting, and the other choices are different biological processes.

questions.prompt: Which seed is best adapted to be spread by water?
questions.questionText: Which seed is best adapted to be spread by water?
choices: A coconut that can float
A burr with hooks
A seed with parachute-like fibers
A seed that stays sealed inside dry soil
options: A coconut that can float
A burr with hooks
A seed with parachute-like fibers
A seed that stays sealed inside dry soil
questions.correctAnswer: A coconut that can float
questions.explanation: Floating helps water carry the seed to a new place. Burrs suit animal dispersal, feathery fibers suit wind dispersal, and the last option is not adapted for spreading.

questions.prompt: A cat runs to the kitchen whenever it hears the can opener because food usually follows. This behavior is:
questions.questionText: A cat runs to the kitchen whenever it hears the can opener because food usually follows. This behavior is:
choices: conditioning
instinct
habituation
regeneration
options: conditioning
instinct
habituation
regeneration
questions.correctAnswer: conditioning
questions.explanation: The can opener sound has become linked with food, which is conditioning. Habituation is ignoring harmless signals, instinct is inherited, and regeneration is body regrowth.

questions.prompt: If a pea plant with genotype Tt is crossed with another Tt plant, what percentage of the offspring are expected to be homozygous recessive?
questions.questionText: If a pea plant with genotype Tt is crossed with another Tt plant, what percentage of the offspring are expected to be homozygous recessive?
questions.explanation: A Tt × Tt cross gives TT, Tt, Tt, and tt, so one out of four is homozygous recessive. The larger percentages do not match the Punnett square.

questions.prompt: Which genotype is homozygous dominant?
questions.questionText: Which genotype is homozygous dominant?
choices: BB
Bb
bb
B/b
options: BB
Bb
bb
B/b
questions.correctAnswer: BB
questions.explanation: Homozygous dominant means both alleles are dominant, so BB fits. Bb is heterozygous, bb is homozygous recessive, and the last option is not the standard answer here.

questions.prompt: Why is vegetative reproduction useful for gardeners growing identical plants?
questions.questionText: Why is vegetative reproduction useful for gardeners growing identical plants?
choices: It can produce new plants with the same traits as the parent
It always creates more genetic variation than seeds
It requires pollination from another plant first
It happens only in microscopic organisms
options: It can produce new plants with the same traits as the parent
It always creates more genetic variation than seeds
It requires pollination from another plant first
It happens only in microscopic organisms
questions.correctAnswer: It can produce new plants with the same traits as the parent
questions.explanation: Vegetative reproduction often creates genetically similar offspring, which helps keep desired traits. The other choices incorrectly describe variation, pollination, or where the process occurs.

questions.prompt: A lizard can regrow part of its tail after injury. Which process is most closely related?
questions.questionText: A lizard can regrow part of its tail after injury. Which process is most closely related?
choices: regeneration
budding
binary fission
conditioning
options: regeneration
budding
binary fission
conditioning
questions.correctAnswer: regeneration
questions.explanation: Regeneration is regrowing a lost body part. The other choices refer to different reproductive or learning processes.

questions.prompt: A school of fish stops reacting when a harmless boat passes by every afternoon. This is an example of:
questions.questionText: A school of fish stops reacting when a harmless boat passes by every afternoon. This is an example of:
choices: habituation
observational learning
courtship
binary fission
options: habituation
observational learning
courtship
binary fission
questions.correctAnswer: habituation
questions.explanation: Habituation means an organism learns to ignore a repeated harmless stimulus. The other choices involve copying behavior, attracting mates, or cell reproduction.

questions.prompt: Which statement about courtship behavior is most accurate?
questions.questionText: Which statement about courtship behavior is most accurate?
choices: It can include signals that help animals choose mates
It is the same as habituation to harmless sounds
It happens only after offspring are born
It is a kind of asexual reproduction
options: It can include signals that help animals choose mates
It is the same as habituation to harmless sounds
It happens only after offspring are born
It is a kind of asexual reproduction
questions.correctAnswer: It can include signals that help animals choose mates
questions.explanation: Courtship often involves sounds, colors, dances, or scents that help with mate choice. The other choices confuse courtship with unrelated behaviors or wrong reproduction types.

questions.prompt: A bear cub practices climbing and wrestling with its siblings. Why can this be useful?
questions.questionText: A bear cub practices climbing and wrestling with its siblings. Why can this be useful?
choices: Playing helps it build skills it may need later
Playing changes its genotype
Playing guarantees it will always survive
Playing is the same thing as seed dispersal
options: Playing helps it build skills it may need later
Playing changes its genotype
Playing guarantees it will always survive
Playing is the same thing as seed dispersal
questions.correctAnswer: Playing helps it build skills it may need later
questions.explanation: Play can help young animals practice movement, coordination, and social skills. It does not change genotype, guarantee survival, or relate to seed dispersal.

questions.prompt: Which statement best describes binary fission?
questions.questionText: Which statement best describes binary fission?
choices: One parent cell divides into two offspring cells
A plant grows a new individual from a root or stem
Two parents combine traits to form offspring
An animal performs a mating display
options: One parent cell divides into two offspring cells
A plant grows a new individual from a root or stem
Two parents combine traits to form offspring
An animal performs a mating display
questions.correctAnswer: One parent cell divides into two offspring cells
questions.explanation: Binary fission is direct splitting of one cell into two cells. The other choices describe vegetative reproduction, sexual reproduction, and courtship.

questions.prompt: Which plant strategy best reduces competition between parent and offspring?
questions.questionText: Which plant strategy best reduces competition between parent and offspring?
choices: Dispersing seeds away from the parent plant
Keeping every seed attached to the parent stem
Producing only one seed each year
Preventing pollination
options: Dispersing seeds away from the parent plant
Keeping every seed attached to the parent stem
Producing only one seed each year
Preventing pollination
questions.correctAnswer: Dispersing seeds away from the parent plant
questions.explanation: Seed dispersal spreads offspring into new spaces with access to light, water, and nutrients. The other choices would not help reproduction or reduce competition as effectively.

questions.prompt: If a flower's purple color is dominant and a plant's genotype is Pp, which statement is true?
questions.questionText: If a flower's purple color is dominant and a plant's genotype is Pp, which statement is true?
choices: The plant's phenotype is purple
The plant's phenotype must be white
The plant is homozygous recessive
The plant has no genotype
options: The plant's phenotype is purple
The plant's phenotype must be white
The plant is homozygous recessive
The plant has no genotype
questions.correctAnswer: The plant's phenotype is purple
questions.explanation: Because P is dominant, the Pp genotype produces the purple phenotype. The other choices ignore dominance or misuse genetic vocabulary.

questions.prompt: Which parent behavior is most likely to protect offspring from predators?
questions.questionText: Which parent behavior is most likely to protect offspring from predators?
choices: A bird pretending to have a broken wing to distract danger from the nest
A mushroom releasing spores into the air
A rabbit becoming used to a harmless fence
A bacterium reproducing by binary fission
options: A bird pretending to have a broken wing to distract danger from the nest
A mushroom releasing spores into the air
A rabbit becoming used to a harmless fence
A bacterium reproducing by binary fission
questions.correctAnswer: A bird pretending to have a broken wing to distract danger from the nest
questions.explanation: The distraction display directly protects offspring by drawing predators away. The other choices involve reproduction or learning but do not protect young from predators.

questions.prompt: Why is a Punnett square useful?
questions.questionText: Why is a Punnett square useful?
choices: It predicts possible allele combinations and trait probabilities in offspring
It shows exactly what every offspring will look like
It proves acquired traits are inherited
It measures how fast learned behaviors develop
options: It predicts possible allele combinations and trait probabilities in offspring
It shows exactly what every offspring will look like
It proves acquired traits are inherited
It measures how fast learned behaviors develop
questions.correctAnswer: It predicts possible allele combinations and trait probabilities in offspring
questions.explanation: Punnett squares organize possible allele pairings and estimate probabilities. They do not guarantee exact outcomes or measure learning and acquired traits.

questions.prompt: A recessive smooth-seed trait is represented by s. If two wrinkled-seed plants both have genotype Ss, what is the probability of a smooth-seed offspring?
questions.questionText: A recessive smooth-seed trait is represented by s. If two wrinkled-seed plants both have genotype Ss, what is the probability of a smooth-seed offspring?
questions.explanation: An Ss × Ss cross gives SS, Ss, Ss, and ss, so one out of four offspring is smooth-seeded. The other percentages do not match the genotype combinations.

questions.prompt: A scientist studies cloned bacteria and notices that one new disease kills nearly all of them. Which idea best explains this outcome?
questions.questionText: A scientist studies cloned bacteria and notices that one new disease kills nearly all of them. Which idea best explains this outcome?
choices: Low genetic variation can make a population more vulnerable
Asexual reproduction always creates stronger offspring
Courtship behavior failed to protect the bacteria
The bacteria had too many acquired traits
options: Low genetic variation can make a population more vulnerable
Asexual reproduction always creates stronger offspring
Courtship behavior failed to protect the bacteria
The bacteria had too many acquired traits
questions.correctAnswer: Low genetic variation can make a population more vulnerable
questions.explanation: Cloned or very similar offspring share the same weaknesses, so a disease can spread through them easily. The other choices misuse courtship, strength, or acquired traits.

questions.prompt: A parent bird sings and displays bright feathers to attract a mate, then later takes food to hatchlings. Which statement best connects these behaviors?
questions.questionText: A parent bird sings and displays bright feathers to attract a mate, then later takes food to hatchlings. Which statement best connects these behaviors?
choices: The first behavior supports reproduction, and the second supports offspring survival
Both behaviors are examples of habituation
Both behaviors are types of asexual reproduction
The first behavior is acquired, so the second cannot be instinctive
options: The first behavior supports reproduction, and the second supports offspring survival
Both behaviors are examples of habituation
Both behaviors are types of asexual reproduction
The first behavior is acquired, so the second cannot be instinctive
questions.correctAnswer: The first behavior supports reproduction, and the second supports offspring survival
questions.explanation: Courtship helps attract a mate, and feeding young helps those offspring survive. The other choices confuse courtship with habituation or asexual reproduction and make unsupported claims about behavior type.

questions.prompt: A gardener notices that a plant with showy flowers produces fewer seeds when pollinators are absent, even though runners still make a few new plants. What conclusion is best?
questions.questionText: A gardener notices that a plant with showy flowers produces fewer seeds when pollinators are absent, even though runners still make a few new plants. What conclusion is best?
choices: The plant can reproduce sexually through seeds and asexually through vegetative reproduction
The plant can reproduce only through runners
The plant cannot reproduce at all without animals
The runners are a form of courtship behavior
options: The plant can reproduce sexually through seeds and asexually through vegetative reproduction
The plant can reproduce only through runners
The plant cannot reproduce at all without animals
The runners are a form of courtship behavior
questions.correctAnswer: The plant can reproduce sexually through seeds and asexually through vegetative reproduction
questions.explanation: Seed production with pollinators shows sexual reproduction, while runners show vegetative reproduction. The other choices ignore one of the observed processes or misuse behavior vocabulary.

questions.prompt: Why might young animals benefit from a mix of instinct and learned behavior?
questions.questionText: Why might young animals benefit from a mix of instinct and learned behavior?
choices: Instinct helps them respond right away, while learning helps them adjust to specific situations
Instinct and learning are the same thing, so both are unnecessary
Only learned behavior affects survival
Only instinct matters after birth
options: Instinct helps them respond right away, while learning helps them adjust to specific situations
Instinct and learning are the same thing, so both are unnecessary
Only learned behavior affects survival
Only instinct matters after birth
questions.correctAnswer: Instinct helps them respond right away, while learning helps them adjust to specific situations
questions.explanation: Instinct can provide immediate survival behaviors, and learning lets animals improve based on experience. The other choices incorrectly treat instinct and learning as identical or deny the value of one of them.
